name: Aka Manah
description: Aka Manah, also known as Akoman, is a malevolent spirit in Zoroastrianism, representing evil thought and bad purpose. He is the antithesis of Vohu Manah, the Good Mind, and embodies the forces that lead humanity astray from the righteous path. Aka Manah actively works to corrupt individuals, sowing doubt, promoting wickedness, and obscuring the truth. He is a powerful figure in the cosmic battle between good and evil, constantly striving to undermine the divine order established by Ahura Mazda. His influence manifests as negative impulses, immoral desires, and flawed judgment, leading individuals towards destructive choices and ultimately, spiritual ruin. Overcoming Aka Manah requires constant vigilance, self-awareness, and a steadfast commitment to the principles of truth, righteousness, and compassion. He represents the internal struggle against temptation and the importance of cultivating a virtuous mindset.
subtitle: The Spirit of Evil Thought in Zoroastrianism
appearance: Aka Manah is often depicted as a shadowy, formless entity, reflecting the intangible nature of evil thoughts. While not always given a concrete physical form, when visualized, he may appear as a grotesque figure, embodying the ugliness and distortion of a corrupted mind.
habitat: Aka Manah dwells within the minds of individuals susceptible to negative influences, as well as in places where wickedness and deceit thrive. He is a constant presence in the world, seeking opportunities to undermine goodness and promote chaos.
behavior: Aka Manah operates through subtle manipulation, preying on weaknesses and exploiting vulnerabilities. He whispers insidious suggestions, amplifies negative emotions, and distorts perceptions to lead individuals down a path of moral decay. He is relentless in his pursuit of corruption, constantly seeking to undermine the forces of good.

keyMyths.title: The Temptation of Zoroaster
keyMyths.description: In some accounts, Aka Manah attempts to tempt Zoroaster himself, offering worldly power and riches in exchange for abandoning his divine mission. Zoroaster, however, resists the temptation through his unwavering faith and righteous thinking, demonstrating the power of Vohu Manah to overcome evil.

keyMyths.title: The Cosmic Battle
keyMyths.description: Aka Manah plays a key role in the ongoing cosmic battle between Ahura Mazda and Angra Mainyu. He represents one of the primary forces of evil working to disrupt the divine order and plunge the world into darkness. His defeat is essential for the final triumph of good.

keyMyths.title: The Judgment of Souls
keyMyths.description: Aka Manah influences the judgment of souls after death, attempting to weigh down the scales with the burden of evil thoughts and deeds. Only those who have successfully resisted his influence can pass into paradise, while those who succumb are condemned to suffering.

sources.author: Boyce, Mary
sources.work: Zoroastrians: Their Religious Beliefs and Practices
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Zoroastrian beliefs, including the role of Aka Manah in the cosmic struggle between good and evil.
sources.text: A comprehensive overview of Zoroastrian beliefs, including the role of Aka Manah in the cosmic struggle between good and evil.

sources.author: Dhalla, Maneckji Nusservanji
sources.description: A detailed examination of Zoroastrian theological concepts, with specific attention to the nature and function of evil spirits like Aka Manah.
sources.text: A detailed examination of Zoroastrian theological concepts, with specific attention to the nature and function of evil spirits like Aka Manah.

sources.author: Hintze, Almut
sources.work: A Zoroastrian Apocalypse: A Study of Frašō.kərəti and Its Pahlavi Commentary
sources.description: Explores the role of evil forces, including Aka Manah, in the Zoroastrian vision of the end times and the ultimate triumph of good.
sources.text: Explores the role of evil forces, including Aka Manah, in the Zoroastrian vision of the end times and the ultimate triumph of good.

sources.author: Clark, Peter
sources.work: Zoroastrianism: An Introduction to an Ancient Faith
sources.description: Provides an accessible introduction to Zoroastrianism, including discussions of its dualistic cosmology and the role of Aka Manah.
